item.title: Govt fails to check city road chaos
item.description: The repeated failure of successive governments to address the existing chaos on the roads of the capital Dhaka has resulted in lack of passenger-friendly public transport, frequent road accidents and intense traffic congestion....

item.title: Anti-graft work in limbo as no ACC in place
item.description: The operations of the Anti-Corruption Commission have nearly come to a halt as the country’s anti-graft agency has been running without its chairman and commissioners for over one and a half months following their resignation....

item.title: Left leader Abdur Rouf remembered
item.description: Politicians, academics, labour leaders and professionals, on Friday at a commemoration meeting said that leftist leader Abdur Rouf Mukul, in his political life, wanted to establish a discrimination and repression-free Bangladesh....

item.title: MRA workshop emphasises on policies for poverty alleviation, women empowerment
item.description: The Microcredit Regulatory Authority held a workshop to present and review the results of a research titled Microcredit for Poverty Alleviation and Women Empowerment: Impact and Determinations at the MRA Auditorium of Agargaon in Dhaka on Thursday....

item.title: Concrete oven: loss of Dhaka breezeway
item.description: DO WE remember that Dhaka north once appointed a chief heat officer? The move was not symbolic, it reflected a growing crisis. Dhaka, once a city that breathed through its rivers and canals, is rapidly turning into a heat trap. What we are witnessing......Maps

item.title: Intellectual performance and power
item.description: IN THIS era of globalization, many intellectual and professional spaces, such as academic conferences, policy forums, expert panels and even prestigious online communities, have been influenced by how intelligence is presented and showed. People are now......

item.title: Giving education a chance
item.description: I BELIEVE education has never been given a real chance in Bangladesh. It may sound controversial, but I will not shy away from saying this. I am a product of the Bangladeshi education system, and I taught in this system for a long time. I have researched......

item.title: A Bangladesh that breathes
item.description: THERE are moments in the life of a nation when something begins to slip — not loudly, not all at once, but in a quiet erosion of clarity. Institutions continue, economies expand, cities grow denser and louder, yet beneath it all, a deeper disorientation......

item.title: Economic development priorities
item.description: THE economic development priorities of the newly elected government must be strategically aligned with the country’s structural realities, global economic dynamics and long-term development aspirations. As Bangladesh transitions from lower-middle-income to......

item.title: Low internet access will mar digital governance
item.description: DIGITAL governance in a world increasingly dependent on technological advancement is no longer an option but an imperative. But it warrants people’s access to digital means. The latest Bangladesh Bureau of Statistics figure which says that......

item.title: Case backlog demands more than promises
item.description: MORE than 4.04 million cases remaining pending in lower courts, alongside more than half a million in the High Court Division and thousands in the Appellate Division, shows a judiciary under immense and persistent strain. The scale of the......

item.title: Venezuela frees dozens of political prisoners
item.description: Venezuela freed 46 political prisoners on Thursday, detainees’ families said, the latest releases after the fall of autocratic leader Nicolas Maduro....

item.title: Dozen exam candidates abducted in Nigeria
item.description: Fourteen people were abducted in central Nigeria’s Benue state while traveling to sit a university entrance exam, police and witnesses said on Thursday....

item.title: Jubilant crowds throng giant papal mass in Cameroon
item.description: A jubilant crowd gathered in stifling heat Friday for a giant open-air mass by Pope Leo XIV at a stadium in Cameroon’s economic capital Douala, the biggest event of a visit marked by his calls for peace and spat with US president Donald Trump....

item.title: China embassy urges Tokyo to probe threats
item.description: The Chinese embassy in Japan has accused Tokyo of failing to protect its diplomats after it received three threats within a month, as a spat between the Asian neighbours rumbles on....

item.title: War with Pakistan halts school for Afghan border children
item.description: There are no children in the Afghan border village of Barikot, where the school has been smashed by shells and the playground sits deserted after weeks of war with Pakistan....

item.title: Harry, Meghan meet survivors of Bondi Beach attack
item.description: Prince Harry and wife Meghan Markle visited Australia’s Bondi Beach on Friday to meet survivors of last year’s mass shooting at the famed surf spot....

item.title: Back on Earth, Artemis II crew still finding their footing
item.description: Nearly a week after their Pacific splashdown, the astronauts who crewed the Artemis II mission that flew around the Moon told reporters Thursday they have yet to fully grasp the magnitude of the moment....

item.title: Trump characterises Iran war as a ‘little diversion’
item.description: President Donald Trump said Thursday the US war with Iran was a ‘little diversion’ during his second term in power, as recent polling shows the conflict’s unpopularity with the American public....

item.title: 38,000 women, girls killed in Gaza war by end of 2025: UN
item.description: Over 38,000 women and girls were killed in the Gaza war by the end of 2025, the UN estimated Friday, amounting to over half of the 71,000 deaths recorded by the territory’s health ministry....

item.title: Starmer again under fire over ex-envoy to US appointment
item.description: UK prime minister Keir Starmer faced mounting pressure to quit on Friday over his appointment of Peter Mandelson as envoy to Washington, after it emerged the veteran politician had failed security vetting....

item.title: Four held over murder of college student hurled down lift shaft
item.description: Four men have been arrested over the killing of a college student who was thrown into a lift shaft from the roof of an under-construction building in Chattogram city’s Bakalia area on April 12....

item.title: Jamaat to back just causes, resist injustice: amir
item.description: Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami amir and leader of the opposition Shafiqur Rahman on Friday said his party will support the government on all just causes while firmly opposing injustice and any actions that undermine people’s rights....

item.title: Youths must work for lasting dev: Fakhrul
item.description: Local government rural development and cooperatives minister and Bangladesh Nationalist party secretary general Mirza Fakhrul Islam Alamgir on Friday said that the government wanted to work collectively to eradicate poverty, improve the living standards of people and bring about a positive change in their lives....

item.title: India govt plan for more women in parliament fails vote
item.description: A proposal to expand India’s parliament to increase the number of women representatives fell through on Friday after the ruling coalition failed to secure enough votes for it in the lower house....

item.title: US special envoy Harder to arrive today
item.description: United States special envoy for Best Future Generations Charles J Harder is scheduled to arrive in Dhaka today on a three-day visit aimed at advancing strategic cooperation between the United States and Bangladesh in the fields of education and child well-being....Maps

item.title: Sri Lanka energy minister quits as president orders coal probe
item.description: Sri Lanka’s energy minister Kumara Jayakody resigned on Friday after the president ordered an investigation into allegations of large-scale corruption in coal imports, the government said....

item.title: Govt to double scholarship to boost education quality
item.description: The government has mapped out a plan to double the amount of money of existing scholarships for meritorious and marginalised students at all tiers of education, from primary to undergraduate studies....

item.title: GAU holds workshop on automation for speed breeding, year-round crops
item.description: Gazipur Agricultural University hosted a workshop titled Innovative Automation for Speed Breeding and Sustainable Year-Round Crop Production through Agricultural Research in Controlled Environment at the university’s old auditorium on Thursday....

item.title: 5-day maritime safety training concluded at Ctg Port
item.description: The five-day MARCO3 Fundamentals Course organised under the EU CRIMARIO project was concluded at the Chittagong Port Training Centre on Thursday....

item.title: Energy-efficient urban planning urged amid crisis
item.description: The Institute for Planning and Development urged the government to adopt energy-efficient urban planning to ensure sustainable and environment-friendly urbanisation amid the ongoing energy insecurity....

item.title: SM Mohsin’s death anniv today
item.description: Today is the fifth anniversary of death of Ekushey Padak-winning theatre activist, actor and freedom fighter SM Mohsin....

item.title: ULAB hosts ‘Pitfalls of National Consciousness’
item.description: The University of Liberal Arts Bangladesh’s Media Studies and Journalism department is hosting a two-day exhibition titled ‘Pitfalls of National Consciousness’ at the Bangladesh Shilpakala Academy, which began on Friday....

item.title: Shadhona organises workshop ‘Embodied Concepts of Prakriti’
item.description: ‘Shadhona – A Centre for the Advancement of South Asian Culture’, together with the ‘Nrittoshilpi Foundation Bangladesh’, inaugurated a six-month-long workshop titled ‘Embodied Concepts of Prakriti’ at the Bangladesh Shilpakala Academy on Friday....

item.title: Indefinite transport strike begins in Kushtia
item.description: INDEFINITE strike enforced by transport workers has brought bus services in Kushtia to a halt, suspending all internal routes and disrupting daily travel as workers press home demands for equal benefits and permission to operate a union-run bus service....

item.title: Scrap ‘unequal’ trade deal with US: rights body
item.description: Leaders of the Democratic Rights Committee at a rally on Friday called on the Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government to take immediate steps to scrap the ‘unequal’ trade deal signed by the Bangladesh interim government during its tenure with the United States government....

item.title: Unity in diversity our core principle: Nitai
item.description: Cultural affairs minister Nitai Roy Chowdhury on Friday reaffirmed the government’s commitment to building a discrimination-free Bangladesh where diversity is embraced as strength....

item.title: 900 Rohingyas dead, missing in shipwrecks in 2025: UN
item.description: The United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees on Friday said that nearly 900 Rohingya refugees were reported missing or dead in the Andaman Sea and Bay of Bengal in 2025, making it the deadliest year on record for maritime movements in......

item.title: Two more die with measles symptoms
item.description: At least two more children with measles symptoms died, and 1,115 more suspected cases were detected in the 24 hours until 8:00am on Friday, according to the Directorate General of Health Services....

item.title: Mob kills 2 after minor girl found dead
item.description: Two people were killed and one was injured in mob beating at Banparil village under Sadar upazila in Manikganj district on Thursday night following the death of a nine-year old girl....

item.title: Fuel pass fails to shorten queues
item.description: Despite the government’s claim of record-high refined fuel reserves, long queues at filling stations in the capital Dhaka and elsewhere in the country continued on Friday, with the recently introduced ‘fuel pass’ system failing to ease consumer pressure....City & Local Guides

item.title: 370 citizens condemn Shahbagh mob attack calling group homosexual
item.description: University professors, human rights defenders, gender and transgender rights activists, artists, journalists and lawyers among 370 citizens on Friday issued a statement condemning recent mob attack on a group of least eight people, including a transgender, at......

item.title: India reviews Hasina extradition request
item.description: India is examining Bangladesh’s request to extradite former prime minister Sheikh Hasina, who fled to India after being ousted from power during the July mass uprising....
